### load_fasta

>  load_fasta (fasta_path:Union[str,pathlib.Path])

*Load a FASTA file and return a dictionary mapping protein IDs to
sequences.*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>fasta_path</td>
<td>Union</td>
<td>Path to the FASTA file to load.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dictionary mapping protein IDs (record.id) to amino acid
sequences (as strings).</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<a
href="https://github.com/mtinti/protein_cutter/blob/main/protein_cutter/core.py#L92"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### digest

>  digest (sequence:str, protein_id:str, enzyme:str='trypsin',
>              missed_cleavages:int=1, charge_states:list=[1, 2, 3],
>              mass_range:tuple=(800.0, 4000.0), min_pep_length:int=5,
>              max_pep_length:int=35, sort_by_mass:bool=False)

*Digest a protein and add flanking amino acids for each peptide.*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>sequence</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>Protein sequence to digest</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>protein_id</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>Protein identifier (for the DataFrame)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>enzyme</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>trypsin</td>
<td>Enzyme name (default: ‘trypsin’)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>missed_cleavages</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>1</td>
<td>Number of allowed missed cleavages (default: 1)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>charge_states</td>
<td>list</td>
<td>[1, 2, 3]</td>
<td>Charge states for m/z calculation (default: [1, 2, 3])</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>mass_range</td>
<td>tuple</td>
<td>(800.0, 4000.0)</td>
<td>(min, max) monoisotopic mass filter in Da (default: (800.0,
4000.0))</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>min_pep_length</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>5</td>
<td>Minimum peptide length to retain (default: 5)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_pep_length</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>35</td>
<td>Maximum peptide length to retain (default: 35)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>sort_by_mass</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>False</td>
<td>Sort output by monoisotopic mass (default: False)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>DataFrame</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>DataFrame with the following columns:<br>- start_index : int
- Start position in protein sequence<br>- end_index : int - End position
in protein sequence<br>- pep_seq : str - Peptide sequence<br>-
protein_id : str - Protein identifier<br>- pep_length : int - Peptide
length<br>- prev_aa : str - Previous amino acid (or ‘-’ at
N-terminus)<br>- next_aa : str - Next amino acid (or ‘-’ at
C-terminus)<br>- extended_seq : str - Sequence with flanking AAs<br>-
rep_extended_seq : str - Extended sequence with parentheses<br>-
mass_mono : float - Monoisotopic mass (Da)<br>- mass_avg : float -
Average mass (Da)<br>- mz_{z} : float - m/z for each charge state in
charge_states</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
df = digest(
    sequence=protein_dict['P15497'],
    protein_id='P15497',
    enzyme='trypsin',
    missed_cleavages=0,
    sort_by_mass=True
  )
df.head(10)
```

### digest_to_set

>  digest_to_set (sequence:str, enzyme:str='trypsin',
>                     missed_cleavages:int=0,
>                     mass_range:tuple[float,float]=(800.0, 4000.0),
>                     min_pep_length:int=5, max_pep_length:int=35)

\*Lightweight digest that returns only peptide sequences as a set.

This is a memory-efficient alternative to
[`digest()`](https://mtinti.github.io/protein_cutter/core.html#digest)
when only the peptide sequences are needed, without positional or mass
annotations.\*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>sequence</td>
<td>str</td>
<td></td>
<td>Protein sequence to digest</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>enzyme</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>trypsin</td>
<td>Enzyme name (default: ‘trypsin’)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>missed_cleavages</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>0</td>
<td>Number of allowed missed cleavages (default: 0)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>mass_range</td>
<td>tuple</td>
<td>(800.0, 4000.0)</td>
<td>(min, max) monoisotopic mass filter in Da (default: (800.0,
4000.0))</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>min_pep_length</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>5</td>
<td>Minimum peptide length to retain (default: 5)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_pep_length</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>35</td>
<td>Maximum peptide length to retain (default: 35)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>set</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Set of peptide sequences passing the length and mass
filters</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/mtinti/protein_cutter/blob/main/protein_cutter/core.py#L274"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### fasta_to_peptide_set

>  fasta_to_peptide_set (fasta_path:Union[str,pathlib.Path],
>                            enzyme:str='trypsin', missed_cleavages:int=0,
>                            mass_range:tuple[float,float]=(800.0, 4000.0),
>                            min_pep_length:int=5, max_pep_length:int=35,
>                            show_progress:bool=True)

\*Parse a FASTA file and return all unique peptides as a set.

Memory-efficient function that digests proteins on-the-fly without
storing the full protein sequences. Useful for building canonical
peptide reference sets for comparison with experimental data.\*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>fasta_path</td>
<td>Union</td>
<td></td>
<td>Path to the FASTA file to parse</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>enzyme</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>trypsin</td>
<td>Enzyme name (default: ‘trypsin’)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>missed_cleavages</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>0</td>
<td>Number of allowed missed cleavages (default: 0)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>mass_range</td>
<td>tuple</td>
<td>(800.0, 4000.0)</td>
<td>(min, max) monoisotopic mass filter in Da (default: (800.0,
4000.0))</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>min_pep_length</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>5</td>
<td>Minimum peptide length to retain (default: 5)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>max_pep_length</td>
<td>int</td>
<td>35</td>
<td>Maximum peptide length to retain (default: 35)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>show_progress</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Show progress bar with tqdm (default: True)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>set</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Set of all unique peptide sequences from the FASTA
file</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
peptide_set = fasta_to_peptide_set(TEST_DATA / 'test_sequence.fa')
```

    Digesting proteins: 2it [00:00, 11765.23it/s]

### annotate_peptides_inplace

>  annotate_peptides_inplace (file_path:Union[str,pathlib.Path],
>                                 canonical_peptides:set[str],
>                                 sequence_col:str='PEP.StrippedSequence',
>                                 new_col_name:str='is_canonical', sep:str=None,
>                                 show_progress:bool=True)

*Annotate peptides in-place by writing to temp file then replacing.*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>file_path</td>
<td>Union</td>
<td></td>
<td>Path to peptide file (will be modified in-place)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>canonical_peptides</td>
<td>set</td>
<td></td>
<td>Set of canonical peptide sequences</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>sequence_col</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>PEP.StrippedSequence</td>
<td>Column name containing peptide sequences (default:
‘PEP.StrippedSequence’)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>new_col_name</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>is_canonical</td>
<td>Name for the new annotation column (default: ‘is_canonical’)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>sep</td>
<td>str</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Field separator. If None, auto-detects from extension (default:
None)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>show_progress</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>

</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>int</strong></td>
<td></td>

</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

``` python
src = TEST_DATA / "test_spectronaut_pep_out.tsv.bk"
dst = TEST_DATA / "test_spectronaut_pep_out.tsv"

shutil.copy(src, dst)
```
